description: PR-11a readiness-dashboard data-model review (companion to pr11a-readiness-dashboard.md).
method: operator self-audit of cc_spine/dashboard.py + the readiness-dashboard schema + CLI plus a test suite, THEN an INDEPENDENT agent code review dispatched and COMPLETED before commit per governance §16.0. The reviewer traced every emitted field to its source, checked determinism, verified the schema against the emitter by hand, confirmed the committed sample regenerates, and probed the CLI with hostile input.
independent_verdict: accept_with_fixes (1 minor + 2 nit, no majors; all fixed before commit)

independent_findings.problem: a --mock file that is valid JSON but not an object (e.g. [1,2]) is truthy and reaches mock_result.get / readiness_report's (mock_result or {}).get, raising an unhandled AttributeError with a traceback instead of a clean error.
independent_findings.resolution: cmd_dashboard validates isinstance(mock_result, dict) and returns 1 with a clean message; build_dashboard defensively coerces a non-dict mock_result to None. Tests test_non_dict_mock_is_treated_as_no_mock and test_cli_rejects_non_dict_mock_cleanly added.

independent_findings.problem: spaced_repetition.next_review_items and the top-level next_recommended_review are the identical expression due[:10] — duplicated payload that could silently diverge if edited inconsistently.
independent_findings.resolution: documented in the schema: both are the head of the due queue today; next_recommended_review is a reserved slot for a future weakness-ranked recommender (PR-11b+), which is why both names exist.

independent_findings.problem: id lists are capped at 10 while due_now/open_count report full totals, with no explicit truncation signal.
independent_findings.resolution: the schema now states the caps and that due_now/open_count are full totals, so truncation is derivable as total > list length. No new field needed.

overstated_claims_corrected: 'adds no policy' softened to 'adds no READINESS policy' in the module docstring, __init__, and report — the display-only content_scale (proof_scale/exam_scale) classification is this module's one judgment of its own and never touches the verdict/score.

the content-free value-leak guarantee is now TEST-ENFORCED beyond field names + holdout stems: new tests assert no practice-item stem/choice text and no journal correction/one_sentence_rule/trap text appears as a value in the dashboard.
clean_areas_confirmed_by_reviewer: content-free: every emitted field traced to source; the journal's answer-bearing fields (correction/one_sentence_rule/trap/selected_index) are never read — only misconception_id via open_journal
determinism: no clock/random/env/mtime; sorted dicts and sets; uniform round(); both CI gates cmp two builds

faithful composition: readiness verdict/score/complete/weighted_over/hard_blockers/note carried verbatim from readiness_report; no independent score; domain/bank accuracy read from state, only display-rounded

schema vs emitter: by-hand check passes — all required keys present, additionalProperties:false honored (the dashboard drops readiness_report's 'components'), enums/types/ranges and mock:null satisfied by the sample
sample regenerable: build_dashboard(replay(stream, now=5), now=5) == committed sample (test)
scaffold schema-count 9->10 matches the new schema; mock section maps grade_mock's return shape correctly
note: Independent review completed BEFORE this commit (the PR-9 lesson). Accept-with-fixes; all three findings + both overstatement flags applied pre-commit, continuing the honesty discipline (the value-leak claim is now test-enforced rather than asserted).
